claim: Google Stitch now has a design agent that acts like AI Studio for UI design; prompts generate both visual designs and code (React, Next.js)
actionable: Use Stitch's design agent to quickly generate UI prototypes with accompanying component code, eliminating manual design-to-code translation
source_title: Google Stitch Just Became an AI Figma (And It's Free)

claim: Design.md files capture design systems (colors, fonts, styling rules) as code artifacts, enabling reuse across projects and version control
actionable: Extract design.md from Stitch designs; use as canonical design spec for multiple projects to enforce brand consistency
source_title: Google Stitch Just Became an AI Figma (And It's Free)

claim: Stitch can ingest a website URL and extract its design theme (colors, fonts) to replicate or remix the aesthetic in new designs
actionable: Use URL-based design inspiration in Stitch; no need to manually map color schemes when AI can extract and apply them
source_title: Google Stitch Just Became an AI Figma (And It's Free)

claim: Stitch integrates with AI Studio for code generation, Figma for export, and MCP servers for agent integration, enabling end-to-end design-to-deployment
actionable: Use Stitch as part of a full-stack agent workflow; designs flow directly into AI Studio for full-stack implementation
source_title: Google Stitch Just Became an AI Figma (And It's Free)

claim: Voice design mode uses Gemini Live for bidirectional conversation; users can talk to Stitch and see changes in real time
actionable: Try voice design for rapid iteration; talking often surfaces design preferences faster than typing detailed specs
source_title: Google Stitch Just Became an AI Figma (And It's Free)

claim: Stitch is free; token costs are covered by Google, making it cost-effective compared to Figma subscriptions
actionable: Evaluate Stitch as a free alternative to Figma for AI-assisted design; cost savings can be substantial for small teams
source_title: Google Stitch Just Became an AI Figma (And It's Free)
